steps.title: Identify the given equation
steps.content: The given equation is $m = 8(n + p)$. The question asks which choice correctly gives $m$ in terms of $n$ and $p$ — that is, which matches the given equation.

steps.title: Compare each choice to the given equation
steps.content:
• Choice A — $m = \frac{8(n+p)}{13}$: Divides by 13, which is not in the original equation. ✗
  • Choice B — $m = 8(n + p)$: Identical to the given equation. ✓
  • Choice C — $m = 8(n + p) - 13$: Subtracts 13, which is not in the original equation. ✗
  • Choice D — $m = 8 - n - p - 13$: Completely changes the structure; distributes incorrectly and subtracts extra terms. ✗
That matches Choice B — $m = 8(n + p)$.
